classification.cautionReason: Conciliar or creedal material received in mainstream Nicene Christian tradition, with denominational differences on some canons.

metadata.author: The Canons of the Councils of Ancyra, Gangra, Neocæsarea, Antioch and Laodicea, which Canons were Accepted and Received by the Ecumenical Synods

title: Canon XXII.

verses.book: The Canons of the Council of Ancyra.
verses.text: Concerning wilful murderers let them remain prostrators; but at the end of life let them be indulged with full communion.

verses.book: The Canons of the Council of Ancyra.
The Canons of the Council of Ancyra.
verses.text: A voluntary homicide may at the last attain perfection.

verses.book: The Canons of the Council of Ancyra.
verses.text: It is noteworthy how singularly appositely [Constantine] Harmenopulus the Scholiast in the Epitom.

verses.book: The Canons of the Council of Ancyra.
The Canons of the Council of Ancyra.
verses.text: 3, tells the following story: “In the time of the Patriarch Luke, a certain bishop gave absolution in writing to a soldier who had committed voluntary homicide, after a very short time of penance; and afterwards when he was accused before the synod of having done so, he defended himself by citing the canon which gives bishops the power of remitting or increasing the length of their penance to penitents.

verses.book: The Canons of the Council of Ancyra.
verses.text: But he was told in answer that this was granted indeed to pontiffs but not that they should use it without examination, and with too great lenity.

verses.book: The Canons of the Council of Ancyra.
verses.text: Wherefore the synod subjected the soldier to the canonical penance and the bishop it mulcted for a certain time, bidding him cease from the exercise of his ministry.”
